diff_scope_note: In --changed-files mode this inspector emits findings tagged with introduced_in_diff: bool per AC-12.1 Mechanism (ii). Only the two listed files are in scope. Pre-existing findings from mandatory script runs (orphaned scripts, agents, commands, duplicate content) are included in full-repo sections but tagged introduced_in_diff: false and are advisory-only per the default-safe rule.

orphaned_subagents.note: Pre-existing; not introduced by this diff. Non-blocking per default-safe rule.

diff_specific_findings.reason: Module docstring says 'All 13 steps run in one Python process' but the diff adds Step 1b (chdir) as a substep between Step 1 and Step 2. The count-based wording is now fragile. The major steps still run Step 1 through Step 13 in comments; Step 1b is a substep, not a full step. The docstring count wording should avoid numeric counts to prevent future staleness.
diff_specific_findings.recommendation: Update docstring line 5 from 'All 13 steps run in one Python process' to 'All validation, sentinel, and exec operations run in one Python process' to avoid fragile step-count wording.
diff_specific_findings.codex_disposition: applied — codex proposed equivalent wording change; Finding 3 confirmed as minor nit with correct characterization of Step 1b as substep.

diff_specific_findings.reason: The Exit codes table describes push.sh wrapper behavior. The diff adds --repo-root passing to execute-push.py which can produce its own exit 2 on OSError during chdir. execute-push.py already self-documents this in its own docstring (line 22). The push.md exit-code table is intentionally push.sh-scoped, so this is NOT a cleanliness violation — it is an optional clarity note only.
diff_specific_findings.recommendation: No action required. Optional: if push.md's exit-code table is intended to cover execute-push.py outcomes as well as push.sh, broaden exit 2 description to 'Blocked option (force/delete/ref-rewrite) OR execute-push.py infrastructure failure (invalid --repo-root, missing push.sh)'. Otherwise no change needed because execute-push.py already documents this.
diff_specific_findings.codex_disposition: applied — codex confirmed false positive if table is push.sh-scoped; demoted to advisory.

non_findings.reason: Note paragraph explaining 'python3 is used directly (no venv activation needed)' is pre-existing and is now consistent with the diff which removed 'source venv/bin/activate &&' from the Step 5 bash snippets. No violation.
non_findings.codex_disposition: applied — codex confirmed this should be a non-finding.

non_findings.item: execute-push.py main(argv: list = None) argv-default behavior
non_findings.reason: Pattern is pre-existing and unrelated to this diff. Not introduced in diff.
non_findings.codex_disposition: applied — codex confirmed this should be a non-finding in --changed-files mode.

non_findings.item: Orphaned scripts in scripts/todo/ and scripts/install/
non_findings.reason: All pre-existing; none introduced by commands/push.md or scripts/execute-push.py changes.

non_findings.item: Duplicate content in projects/ and worktrees/
non_findings.reason: Pre-existing runtime artifacts; not introduced by this diff.

summary.verdict: No blockers. One minor docstring wording nit in execute-push.py (introduced_in_diff: true). One advisory clarity note for push.md exit-code table scope (introduced_in_diff: true, non-blocking). All other findings from mandatory script runs are pre-existing and advisory-only.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex reviewed all four draft findings. It confirmed: Finding 1 (exit-code table doc gap) is over-scoped / false positive since execute-push.py already self-documents exit 2 and the push.md table is intentionally push.sh-scoped — proposed reword to advisory-only. Finding 2 (venv note) should be a non-finding since the note is pre-existing and now consistent — proposed move to non-findings. Finding 3 (docstring step count) is an acceptable minor nit but recharacterized: Step 1b is a substep, the real problem is fragile count wording — proposed wording change to avoid counts. Finding 4 (argv default) should be a non-finding as it is pre-existing and unrelated. Codex also noted: no proposed deletion in the draft so no Safety Rules violation. Summary was overstated and corrected.

findings.issue: Finding 1 over-scoped / false positive: execute-push.py already documents exit 2 for --repo-root at line 22; push.md exit-code table is push.sh-scoped, so no cleanliness finding if that scoping is intentional.
findings.proposed_fix: Reword as advisory only: optional clarity note if push.md exit-code table is intended to cover execute-push.py as well as push.sh.
findings.rationale: Finding 1 demoted to advisory; not a real cleanliness violation in the changed files.

findings.issue: Finding 2 should not be a finding: the venv note is pre-existing and now consistent with the diff changes.
findings.proposed_fix: Move to Non-findings: push.md venv note is pre-existing and consistent after removal of source venv/bin/activate; no violation.
findings.rationale: Finding 2 removed from findings list; moved to non-findings section.

findings.issue: Finding 3 acceptable as minor nit but Step 1b is a substep not a full step; the fragile count wording is the real problem.
findings.proposed_fix: Change docstring wording to avoid counts: 'All validation, sentinel, and exec operations run in one Python process; os.execv replaces the process image...'
findings.rationale: Finding 3 retained as minor nit but description and recommendation updated to use codex's proposed wording; no longer claims 14 steps.

findings.issue: Finding 4 should not be a finding in --changed-files mode: argv-default behavior is pre-existing and unrelated to this diff.
findings.proposed_fix: Remove from findings or move to non-findings.
findings.rationale: Finding 4 removed from findings list; moved to non-findings section.

findings.issue: No proposed deletion in draft — no Safety Rules deletion violation.
Summary overstated: two findings were non-findings and Finding 1 was optional scope.
findings.proposed_fix: Summary: No blockers. At most one minor docstring wording nit in execute-push.py, plus an optional advisory clarification for the push.md exit-code table if it is intended to cover execute-push.py.
findings.rationale: Summary rewritten to accurately reflect post-codex findings.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied all four PROPOSED_FIX items: Finding 1 demoted to advisory-only, Finding 2 moved to non-findings, Finding 3 retained as minor with corrected description and codex's proposed wording for the fix, Finding 4 moved to non-findings. Summary rewritten.
